# Target layout in Word and LibreOffice

## Shared rule (both platforms)

**Canonical text** uses Unicode Kanbun from marinaMoji (`說㆒㆑者`).  
**Presentation** is generated by the host app’s renderer. Never treat presentation alone as the archival format.

See [ARCHITECTURE.md](ARCHITECTURE.md) and [LIBREOFFICE_FRAMES.md](LIBREOFFICE_FRAMES.md).

## LibreOffice Writer — primary (frame-based)

### Professional renderer: anchored frame

Used for **simple and compound** kaeriten.

- Borderless frame, **anchor as character** after the annotated kanji.
- Stacked glyphs inside frame (e.g. `㆒㆑` → 一 over レ).
- Bottom-aligned content, ~0.2 mm offset, small font (see frame doc for parameters).
- Works in **vertical text**.

Triggered by extension: **Render kaeriten** (selection / paragraph / document), not ruby.

### Fallback: character styling

- Unicode ㆑ or subscript レ, reduced size.
- Use when extension unavailable or for trivial single レ only.
- **Insufficient** for compound kaeriten (一レ, 上レ, etc.).

### Rejected: Asian ruby

- Phonetic guide API places text like **furigana**, not kaeriten.
- Cannot naturally stack compound marks.

## Microsoft Word — later phase

Same canonical source; **different renderer** (no LO frame import).

### Level 1 — fallback (subscript)

1. Find Unicode mark after base kanji.
2. Replace with subscript レ / 一 / 二 per [mapping.json](../mapping.json).

Reference: [送り仮名とレ点](https://www.shuiren.org/chuden/toyoshi/kanbun/01.htm).

### Level 2 — quality (fields)

- Ruby + subscript レ on same line via field code editing.
- EQ fields for left/right okurigana + レ — [ふること](https://furukoto.hateblo.jp/entry/2021/05/04/000335).
- 再読: [再読文字](https://www.shuiren.org/chuden/toyoshi/kanbun/03.htm) — fragile; use sparingly.

Compound kaeriten in Word may need EQ overlay similar in spirit to LO frames (TBD).

## Okurigana (both platforms, later)

**送り仮名** → ruby (ルビ), katakana in dialog — separate from kaeriten renderer.

## 訓点マクロ (historical contrast)

Tag → batch convert to ruby/subscript. marinaMoji skips tags; source is already `㆒㆑`.  
篠原 (2024): direct subscript/ruby is simpler for basic marks; tags mainly help 再読 in Word.

## 漢文エディタ import (optional)

`登る[レ]山に。` ↔ `登ル^山ニ。` — importer to Unicode source, then same render pipeline.

## TeX (export from source, not from frames)

`kunten2e.sty`, `sfkanbun-lua.sty` — [SOURCES.md](SOURCES.md). Exporters read canonical `說㆒㆑者`, not LO frame objects.
